Two strings are anagrams if and only if their character counts (or their sorted strings) are equal.
Consider what makes a good hash map key. A sorted string works, but takes `O(K log K)` time to sort, where `K` is the string length.
Instead of sorting, you can build an array of 26 counts (one for each letter `a-z`) and use that as the key.
In JavaScript/TypeScript, you can easily turn an array of 26 counts into a string (e.g. `countArr.join('#')`) and use it as a map key!
